He also was recently the head of the defense steering committee and lead counsel for a complex construction injury case with over 30 parties, and catastrophic injuries. He has also been retained in complex litigation including the collapse of a tent resulting in multiple fatalities and 19 plaintiffs claims.  He also acts as a litigation consultant, billing fees expert, and expert in litigation of construction defect disputes.  He has recently been retained as an expert witness in multiple party litigation in California, Texas, Florida, Arizona, among other states.   Dan also maintains an active practice in consulting with carriers on litigation management strategy, implementation, and improvement. I decided to become a Doctor when I was ten, and while the interests and priorities that shaped my career have changed over the years, my reason for being has not--to help others.  As a practicing Pain Medicine Physician, I feel honored to be part of each patient's journey back to a vibrant and rewarding life, but it is sobering to know that whatever I do within the walls of my clinic is not enough.  Healthcare is confusing, frustrating, expensive, and anxiety provoking.  These are symptoms of a broken system, where government, industry, and health systems clash, and the patient is the ultimate loser.  This cannot continue! This reality is why I have re-focused my energy to reimagine healthcare, not as a spin-off of what it is, but rather what it should be.  This purpose has lead me down the path to medical directorships, an MBA from Isenberg School of Management, and leadership training culminating as a Certified Physician Executive.  Many of the answers to making healthcare approachable, affordable, and high-quality are right in front of our eyes!  Other industries have shown us the way, and we just need to be the "innovation brokers" to move the needle for healthcare.  This is where I am going . . .

Gregory Gurbikian is the co-founder and President/CEO of Healthcare Solutions Direct, LLC which primarily services the Senior Health Insurance Market. Understanding that Senior Health Insurance can be confusing to most individuals, Greg has brought over 10 years of experience in the industry and is dedicated to simplifying the process for both its customers and employees.   Under his leadership and vision, Healthcare Solutions Direct, LLC has become one of the nations top agencies servicing the more than 44 million beneficiaries on Medicare. With close to 11,000 people a day turning 65, those on Medicare are projected to rise to over 79 million by 2030. Healthcare Solutions Direct, LLC is poised with cutting edge technology and training to help service the transition of those going onto Medicare from start to finish. Healthcare Solutions Direct, LLC is committed to making sure our customers and those who work for us will have a positive experience they will never forget.  When not working at the office Greg likes to travel, play golf, and spend time with his wife and two kids.  Healthcare Solutions Direct, LLC is based in Tampa, FL and was founded in 2014 with a vision to operate as a company based on integrity, educating the customer, and providing outstanding customer service to our customers nationwide. We want the best for our customers and those who work for us because the Medicare Supplement market can seem very confusing and overwhelming. We are committed to make the process very simple and easy to understand.  We represent the Major Medicare Insurance Carriers in the United States, allowing us to pinpoint which one is best for our customers. Our company’s growth and success are evident by the outstanding sales professionals that take their time to help educate the customer on what is the best choice to meet their specific needs.

headline: Dr. Kirk Tamaddon is the Area Medical Director and Chief of Staff at Kaiser Permanente West Los Angeles Medical Center.    As Area Medical Director and Chief of Staff, he has responsibility for the care delivered by 535 physicians (and 1900 employees) to more than 241,000 Kaiser Permanente member patients at a 252-bed hospital and 7 medical office satellite buildings with a $492 million dollar budget. Dr. Tamaddon is accountable for all aspects which impact patient care, physician practice and business success including Quality of Care, Care Experience, Access, Financial Performance, Leadership Development, Physician Support and HR.  He prepares the medical center for future success in the competitive Los Angeles healthcare market  through recruiting top talent, exploration, use of new innovations, technologies and change management efforts.    In 2020 and 2021, Dr. Tamaddon navigated Kaiser Permanente West Los Angeles successfully and safely through the COVID-19 Pandemic in one of the hardest hit communities and cities in the United States.  He has led a leadership team that has received recognition for psychological safety and a high performance culture by developing a more inclusive and diverse work force and reducing care disparities among ethnically diverse patient populations.   Dr. Tamaddon has been with Kaiser Permanente for the past 26 years.  He received General Surgery and Urologic Surgery Residency training at KP, Los Angeles Medical Center.   He then started his attending staff career at KP West Los Angeles where he developed the first Minimally Invasive Surgery Program.  He was a pioneering founder and led the development and growth of the first Robotic Surgery Program at Kaiser Permanente. He also served as the Chief of the Department of Urologic Surgery.  He has further expertise and training in Physician Clinical Coding and is a Certified Professional Coder (CPC) and led the KP SCAL Physician Documentation and Coding Programs for 13 years prior to his current role.     Dr. Tamaddon credits much of his success in life to his wife Fedra (a school teacher) and their 3 children, Darius, Cameron and Saam who also keep him active as avid ping-pong and tennis players.

headline: Dr. Christopher Piorkowski serves as the divisional vice president and chief medical officer of the electrophysiology (EP) division at Abbott. He graduated in medicine from Charite Berlin and trained in internal medicine, cardiology and EP at the Heart Center Leipzig, Germany.   Prior to joining Abbott, Dr. Piorkowski established and led the Department of EP at the University of Dresden, elevating it to an internationally acclaimed center of medical excellence. With a portfolio of over 275 scientific publications, Dr. Piorkowski has had a significant impact on the field of EP, pioneering innovative ablation techniques and driving medical technology advancements.  Throughout his clinical journey, he has extended his expertise to patients & physicians across Europe, Asia, and South America. He was elected as the President of the Arrhythmia Working Group of the German Cardiac Society and served as a member of the EP certification committee in the European Heart Rhythm Association. Dr. Piorkowski has also lent his insights as a scientific reviewer for various influential EP journals and medical institutions.   You can find some of his favorite publications here:  1. https://evidence.nejm.org/doi/full/10.1056/EVIDoa2200141 2. https://academic.oup.com/europace/article/22/12/1812/5896139?login=true 3. https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/full/10.1161/circep.117.005748 4. https://www.heartrhythmjournal.com/article/S1547-5271(08)01203-4/fulltext 5. https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/full/10.1161/CIRCEP.121.010011 6. https://academic.oup.com/europace/article/20/FI_3/f312/4983994?login=true 7. https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/10.1161/CIRCEP.112.973719

position: Medical Director, Owner | Clinical Operations Consultant & Coach
headline: The patient I'm most concerned with is the medical provider. Too many of us are suffering under endless administrative burden and relentless clinical demands. As a nurse practitioner working in home-based geriatric care and an operations consultant across ambulatory settings, I’ve seen firsthand how improved clinician wellbeing transforms patient outcomes.   So, I partner with clinicians, practice leaders, and patients to design systems and care plans that are personal, data-driven, and grounded in purpose.  My work focuses on reducing administrative burden and rebuilding operational clarity so providers can return to what matters: presence, precision, and care. We focus on quick wins to start, such as clearer workflows, smarter AI use, and stronger boundaries. And then we tackle larger structural and cultural issues to overcome poor engagement and optimize quality outcomes.   I help teams translate complexity into sustainable systems by connecting technology with the bedside, coaching clinicians through burnout recovery, and aligning practice operations with the human rhythms of medicine. Whether I’m shortening and standardizing a charting process, advising a solo practice on automation, or guiding a clinician back to professional joy, my goal is the same: to make care more efficient, more personal, and more possible.  If you’re seeking a way to do good work well without losing yourself in the process, I’d be honored to help you find it.

headline: Dr. Robert T. Adamson is the Executive Vice President and Chief Information Officer for RWJBarnabas Health. In this role he is responsible for strategic planning, technology leadership and vision for the health system and enabling information technology initiatives to execute health system strategic imperatives. He also maintains the role of Operational Leader for The EPIC Electronic Health Record implementation. In this current role he leads all operational aspects of the Epic implementation and provides strategic direction and overall management of the Epic implementation project.   Prior to taking these roles he served as the Senior Vice President and Chief Pharmacy Officer for RWJBarnabas Health. His responsibilities included strategic planning for the pharmacy enterprise, designing, managing, and improving the medication use system ensuring quality outcomes through performance-improvement activities, and fulfill the organization’s research and educational missions. In addition, he ensured that information systems and technology used in pharmacy and patient care environments maximizes the safety, effectiveness, and efficiency of medication prescribing, dispensing, and administration as well as manages the pharmacy enterprise financial performance  After earning a Bachelor of Science degree in pharmacy from St. John's College of Pharmacy, he earned his Doctor of Pharmacy degree from Rutgers University College of Pharmacy, where he is currently an adjunct instructor.   Dr. Adamson has conducted several disease research studies in the areas of infectious disease, oncology and oncology supportive care, and various internal medicine subjects.  He is a recipient of the Greg Santora New Jersey Pharmacist of the Year Award and was bestowed fellowship designation in 2014 by the American Society of Health System Pharmacists, among a wealth of professional awards and honors. He has published widely in prestigious medical journals, serves on the editorial board of major periodicals in his field and has presented at more than 500 medical conferences nationally, internationally as well as on television.

Dr. Clarissa Barnes is a physician leader and healthcare architect committed to improving the practice of medicine for both patients and healthcare workers. Dr. Barnes’ career as public servant has been defined by her philosophy of leading from the front. Adept at identifying pain points, she has made it her mission to ease burdens by finding innovative solutions to inefficiencies. She is a fierce advocate for driving positive change in the medical profession and making systems that work better for everyone.Clarissa melded her interest in the humanities and the sciences by obtaining her BA in both political science and chemistry from the University of South Dakota summa cum laude. She received her medical degree from The Johns Hopkins School of Medicine and then completed an internal medicine residency at the Johns Hopkins Hospital. She later completed her MBA at Creighton University’s Heider College of Business.  As an internist Clarissa has held numerous leadership positions where she has been recognized for her approachability, resourcefulness and willingness to champion systems improvements that benefit patients and clinicians.  She was medical director of the clinician wellness program for Avera Health System across five states and she successfully led a coalition to amend the South Dakota Board of Medicine’s rules that deterred physicians from accessing mental health services. She spent five years improving workflow through her roles as system physician advisor for the Avera Health System and lead physician advisor for Avera McKennan Hospital with significant fiscal impacts. She is currently the Chief Medical Officer for Medicaid for the state of South Dakota.  She is charged with overseeing healthcare quality and population health for Medicaid recipients in South Dakota in order to promote innovative, high-quality, cost-effective healthcare services that promote overall well-being, quality of life, and economical and efficient care.She is the immediate past President of the American College of Physician Advisors, the professional non-profit association for physicians and other clinicians involved in case management, hospital utilization, clinical documentation integrity, health system revenue cycle, and healthcare regulation.  She continues to practice clinically as a hospitalist as well as teach and is a Clinical Professor in the Department of Internal Medicine at the University of South Dakota.

Dr. McDonald, a board-certified internist, joined the American Board of Internal Medicine (ABIM) in 2014 after serving as Associate Chair of the Department of Medicine and Residency Program Director at the Mayo Clinic, Rochester, MN, one of the nation’s largest internal medicine residencies. While at the Mayo Clinic, he led the program's participation in the Accreditation Council for Graduate Medical Education’s (ACGME’s) Educational Innovations Project, which investigated many concepts that would eventually become part of the Next Accreditation System. Since joining ABIM, he has continued his role in patient care and GME as an attending physician of the J. Edwin Wood Clinic and faculty of the Pennsylvania Hospital in Philadelphia.  As a longtime member of the Association of Program Directors in Internal Medicine (APDIM), Dr. McDonald chaired the APDIM Survey Committee from 2007 to 2011, leading the development of longitudinally tracked surveys addressing areas of importance in GME. He was a member of the ACGME Residency Review Committee for Internal Medicine (RRC-IM) during the transition to the Next Accreditation System.  He earned an undergraduate degree in physics with highest distinction (grade point average) and with highest honors from the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill, where he was a Morehead Scholar.  He went on to train as a Howard Hughes Medical Institute/National Institutes of Health (NIH) Research Scholar at NIH’s Bethesda campus. He earned his medical degree from Mayo Medical School and completed an internal medicine internship on the Osler Medical Service of The Johns Hopkins Hospital. He completed internal medicine residency at Mayo Clinic in Rochester, Minn., where he also served as Chief Resident. He later earned a master of public health degree from the Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health, while working as a full-time hospitalist at the Mayo Clinic.  Dr. McDonald attained the rank of Adjunct Professor of Medicine at both the Mayo Clinic College of Medicine and Science and the Perelman School of Medicine at the University of Pennsylvania.  Dr. McDonald has been certified in Internal Medicine by ABIM since 2000. In addition to being a longtime, proud member of the Alliance for Academic Internal Medicine and APDIM, he is a fellow of the American College of Physicians, a member of the Society of Hospital Medicine, and active with the ACGME and its RRC-IM, on which he served first as a voting member and then as ABIM’s ex officio member.

Michael A. Hecht, O.D. is the Chief Medical Officer and Director of Provider Relations of Premier Eye Care, a leading third party administrator of eye care benefits for health plans. For almost 30 years, Premier has helped health plan partners manage risks while insuring that their members receive high-quality, cost-effective care.  Dr. Hecht began his career with Premier Eye Care in 1993. After graduating from the University of Miami with a B.S. in Biology in 1976, he entered the Pennsylvania College of Optometry, where he earned a degree in Visual Sciences in 1977 and Doctor of Optometry in 1980. Immediately following graduation, Dr. Hecht returned to Florida to practice within a large multi-specialty ophthalmology practice in Palm Beach County.   In the early 1980s, Dr. Hecht’s efforts resulted in an integration of primary care optometry and general ophthalmology, fostering an environment of cooperation and communication between the two eye care professions in South Florida. Well recognized for his expertise in developing ophthalmic provider networks and in the clinical aspects of ophthalmic practices, he was invited to join and assist in the establishment of Premier Eye Care.  In addition to his role as Chief Medical Officer of Premier Eye Care, Dr. Hecht was a member of the Palm Beach County Optometric Society and served as President from 1986-1987. He was also the Chairman of the Palm Beach Winter Seminar, serving from 1990-1999.  Premier Eye Care, ranked as one of “Florida’s Best Companies to Work For” for six consecutive years, has also been ranked as one of the state’s fastest-growing companies by the Florida Business Journals.  Premier manages $150M in full-risk medical and routine ophthalmic care nationally and is recognized for industry-leading technology-based solutions and a successful corporate culture that encourages innovation and self-direction to team members, half of whom are millennials.
